--- Thread (44 messages, 2021-01-31) ---
[2021-01-31 21:27] cooledcannon: i mean the black card progress from points themselves not the armory rewards
[2021-01-31 21:30] zera777 (Zera): % per point (based on SpiritFryer's data)
25: 0.2
100: 0.25
175: 0.28571428
250: 0.3
It doesn't scale linearly. The more points you get, the more % each point is worth.
[2021-01-31 21:32] cooledcannon: not what i'm asking if i'm parsing this right
[2021-01-31 21:32] cooledcannon: like before you click the rewards
[2021-01-31 21:32] cooledcannon: you get smth in the ballpark of 0.1% per point just for earning those points
[2021-01-31 21:36] zera777 (Zera): Yes, you get 0.1% for each point earned, in addition to what you spend them on.
[2021-01-31 21:38] cooledcannon: I need to check again but I do feel it's in the ballpark but it's not exactly that amnt
[2021-01-31 21:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): looks like it's 0.1%
[2021-01-31 21:42] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): 163 got me from 24.6% to 40.1%
[2021-01-31 21:44] jamberine: yeah I got 106 = 10.1%
[2021-01-31 21:44] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Tested twice and got
0.09529411764705882352941176470588
0.09509202453987730061349693251534
% per point
[2021-01-31 21:45] jamberine: looks like 1050 pts = 1 card?
[2021-01-31 21:45] shadourow (Rando): Can you be more precise, please ?
[2021-01-31 21:45] jamberine: or something like that
[2021-01-31 21:46] cooledcannon: @Elyot? care to weigh in on this not-so-urgent subject matter
[2021-01-31 21:48] elyot: That sounds right, max = 1 card
[2021-01-31 21:48] cooledcannon: ahhhh clever girl spirit
[2021-01-31 21:49] cooledcannon: how did u get this jank tho
[2021-01-31 21:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Windows Calculator.exe
[2021-01-31 21:49] cooledcannon: how can a calc be wrong
[2021-01-31 21:49] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well the actual number is 1050/100 = 0.0952380952380952380952380952381
[2021-01-31 21:50] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But I guess due to the progress bar only showing up to 1 decimal, you get rounding errors
[2021-01-31 21:50] cooledcannon: so i guess rounding errors
[2021-01-31 21:51] cooledcannon: eloyot? is the progress bar having 1 decimal a visual thing, or is it actually in increments of .1%
[2021-01-31 21:51] cooledcannon: i wonder if i should tag him agin
[2021-01-31 21:51] elyot: it's a float that gets rounded to an int
[2021-01-31 21:51] elyot: internally I think it's floats all the way down
[2021-01-31 21:51] elyot: but would have to check code to verify
[2021-01-31 21:52] elyot: standard best practice is
[2021-01-31 21:52] elyot: only round when displaying in the UI, otherwise never round
[2021-01-31 21:53] cooledcannon: fun fact: in jagex's runescape themed... uh idk what genre advance wars/fire emblem is, game, damage is calculated by rounding at every step
[2021-01-31 21:53] cooledcannon: based on class, you deal damage based on 25, 50, 90%, rounded down
[2021-01-31 21:53] awaclus: that fact is not very fun
[2021-01-31 21:53] cooledcannon: then you have terrain defense, which blocks 0, 10, 20, 30, 40% rounded up
[2021-01-31 21:54] cooledcannon: i believe there's an additional step of rounding too
[2021-01-31 21:54] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): you just reminded me
[2021-01-31 21:54] cooledcannon: normally, only melee attacks have the defender counterattack
[2021-01-31 21:54] awaclus: dude, nobody gives a ShiT
[2021-01-31 21:55] cooledcannon: if the defender is ranged, that number is 3x/20, rounded down
[2021-01-31 21:55] cooledcannon: i believe punfie gives a shit
[2021-01-31 21:55] cooledcannon: we discussed this at length
[2021-01-31 21:55] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): only cooledcannon can deliver such cool facts
[2021-01-31 21:56] cooledcannon: another fun fact: this was not told to the players, so it was reverse engineered
[2021-01-31 21:56] cooledcannon: the 3x/20 took some clever figuring out, esp given the rounding

--- Thread (6 messages, 2021-02-26) ---
[2021-02-26 01:57] elyot: thank you for pinging me
[2021-02-26 01:57] elyot: I literally saw the error message in my email
[2021-02-26 01:57] elyot: checked around, thought everything was OK
[2021-02-26 01:57] elyot: Guess I didn't check around enough! So thank you for pinging me.
[2021-02-26 01:57] elyot: Sometimes it's hard to tell a serious error from a one-time blip
[2021-02-26 01:58] elyot: Basically the server lost connection with the database, and it kept trying to reconnect but couldn't. I don't understand why that happens, but restarting the server fixed it. Might be amazon, might be us, but I will look into it further if it happens again

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (45 messages, 2021-04-24) ---
[2021-04-24 21:59] siepu: it's not EA but yes, basically dead
[2021-04-24 21:59] siepu: just the single player is still tons of fun and it's free, so enjoy!
[2021-04-24 22:00] siepu: but if you dabble with multiplayer be warned that while your enemies will be very nice and friendly
[2021-04-24 22:00] siepu: they've all been playing the game for years
[2021-04-24 22:01] siepu: it's still a fantastic game with tons of content to enjoy, welcome and have fun!
[2021-04-24 22:02] megasupp (mega-supp): the game is a good product as-is. Only thing is it doesn't get updates and chapters 4 and 5 of campaign will likely be never finished
[2021-04-24 22:09] awaclus: that's the opposite of true
[2021-04-24 22:10] siepu: dunno the free puzzles and 1st episode are free and really fun (at least they were for me)
[2021-04-24 22:11] awaclus: the single player campaign is the only non-cosmetic part of the game that costs money
[2021-04-24 22:11] awaclus: and the extra puzzles
[2021-04-24 22:11] awaclus: I won't deny that they're fun, but they're also not as fun as the multiplayer
[2021-04-24 23:01] pseudometa: according to Steam its still in Early Access
[2021-04-24 23:02] awaclus: I think it's in Early Access, yes
[2021-04-24 23:02] awaclus: the campaign isn't finished
[2021-04-24 23:03] pseudometa: why didn't the game take off? Great mechanics, actually interesting story, great reviews, multiplayer that seems deep?
[2021-04-24 23:05] pseudometa: the game feels like it has everything to be as successful as Hades or FTL
[2021-04-24 23:05] siepu: naaah it's.. very niche
[2021-04-24 23:06] pseudometa: I dont have the impression that is more niche than FTL. FTL is niche and far more frustrating at the beginning at still was sucessfull
[2021-04-24 23:06] siepu: FTL also was released 5 years earlier
[2021-04-24 23:10] awaclus: it's a complex situation with many factors contributing to it
[2021-04-24 23:12] awaclus: one of the main ones is that it's a free game without p2w
[2021-04-24 23:12] siepu: it's easy to 'have fun' in FTL, Prismata is optimisation: the game
[2021-04-24 23:12] siepu: basically tons of math
[2021-04-24 23:12] siepu: without randomness to blame your results on
[2021-04-24 23:13] awaclus: which means the fanbase doesn't actually spend that much money on it after maybe initially buying one of the tiers to support the game, and so loonark doesn't get enough money to market the game, and so they don't get very many new players, and so they don't get enough money to market the game, etc
[2021-04-24 23:13] siepu: the only game I know comparable to Prismata in it's nerdiness is Dominions (tho for completely opposite reasons)
[2021-04-24 23:14] pseudometa: factorio is also an RNG-free game which is basically 100% optimization. While optimization games will never have a giant playerbase, I feel like there are enough ppl who would play this
[2021-04-24 23:14] siepu: in Factorio you also can 'have fun' building things
[2021-04-24 23:14] siepu: Prismata is basically 1v1 turn based Starcraft
[2021-04-24 23:15] awaclus: in Prismata you can have fun playing 1v1 turn based Starcraft
[2021-04-24 23:15] siepu: imagine Factorio but if you make a mistake you lose everything
[2021-04-24 23:16] pseudometa: so basically bad business model decision? too bad, that the devs are being "punished" for not implementing p2w crap
[2021-04-24 23:16] siepu: I think Prismata is awesome for the audience it's designed for and that audience is bigger than us, just it's still really niche
[2021-04-24 23:16] siepu: (talking MP, puzzles are accessible)
[2021-04-24 23:17] awaclus: it's not necessarily a bad business model decision, because almost everyone who gets far enough to hear what the business model is, loves the business model
[2021-04-24 23:18] awaclus: the problem is that it would probably require more of an initial investment, or preferably be made by a studio that was already known for a few other games
[2021-04-24 23:19] awaclus: this is based on what Elyot has said, for the record
[2021-04-24 23:19] pseudometa: I mean bad business model income-generating wise. I mean their business model is great, but it sounds like a more traditional "pay 20 bucks for the game (multiplayer & singleplayer)" would have been better
[2021-04-24 23:20] pseudometa: Also I cannot really fathom that this genre would be so niche. Prismata is basically competetive puzzling. And there are so many ppl playing puzzle games
[2021-04-24 23:20] megasupp (mega-supp): I wouldn't call prismata competitive puzzling
[2021-04-24 23:21] awaclus: what he has not said (that I know of anyway) but I think is also the case, is that since this is loonark's first game, when it comes to marketing it, they are probably spending resources they don't really have too much of on trying out things that don't end up working
[2021-04-24 23:22] pseudometa: Hmmm, now I wonder how games like FTL became sucessfull. First game, Indie, weird new mechanics, even frsutrating for many beginners.
[2021-04-24 23:27] awaclus: well, sort of, I mean it probably would work out if the playerbase was larger
[2021-04-24 23:28] awaclus: because they would keep recommending it to their friends, and then you would have these people who hear about Prismata from multiple different sources and then eventually decide to check it out
[2021-04-24 23:29] awaclus: but between the game being niche, and the community being small, it doesn't have that effect a lot, outside of perhaps some specific communities

messages.content: it's a complex situation with many factors contributing to it

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: one of the main ones is that it's a free game without p2w

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: it's easy to 'have fun' in FTL, Prismata is optimisation: the game

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: basically tons of math

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: without randomness to blame your results on

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: which means the fanbase doesn't actually spend that much money on it after maybe initially buying one of the tiers to support the game, and so loonark doesn't get enough money to market the game, and so they don't get very many new players, and so they don't get enough money to market the game, etc

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: the only game I know comparable to Prismata in it's nerdiness is Dominions (tho for completely opposite reasons)

messages.author: pseudometa
messages.content: factorio is also an RNG-free game which is basically 100% optimization. While optimization games will never have a giant playerbase, I feel like there are enough ppl who would play this

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: in Factorio you also can 'have fun' building things

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: Prismata is basically 1v1 turn based Starcraft

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: in Prismata you can have fun playing 1v1 turn based Starcraft

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: imagine Factorio but if you make a mistake you lose everything

messages.author: pseudometa
messages.content: so basically bad business model decision? too bad, that the devs are being "punished" for not implementing p2w crap

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: I think Prismata is awesome for the audience it's designed for and that audience is bigger than us, just it's still really niche

messages.author: siepu
messages.content: (talking MP, puzzles are accessible)

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: it's not necessarily a bad business model decision, because almost everyone who gets far enough to hear what the business model is, loves the business model

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: the problem is that it would probably require more of an initial investment, or preferably be made by a studio that was already known for a few other games

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: this is based on what Elyot has said, for the record

messages.author: pseudometa
messages.content: I mean bad business model income-generating wise. I mean their business model is great, but it sounds like a more traditional "pay 20 bucks for the game (multiplayer & singleplayer)" would have been better

messages.author: pseudometa
messages.content: Also I cannot really fathom that this genre would be so niche. Prismata is basically competetive puzzling. And there are so many ppl playing puzzle games

messages.author: megasupp
messages.content: I wouldn't call prismata competitive puzzling

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: what he has not said (that I know of anyway) but I think is also the case, is that since this is loonark's first game, when it comes to marketing it, they are probably spending resources they don't really have too much of on trying out things that don't end up working

messages.author: pseudometa
messages.content: Hmmm, now I wonder how games like FTL became sucessfull. First game, Indie, weird new mechanics, even frsutrating for many beginners.

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: well, sort of, I mean it probably would work out if the playerbase was larger

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: because they would keep recommending it to their friends, and then you would have these people who hear about Prismata from multiple different sources and then eventually decide to check it out

messages.author: awaclus
messages.content: but between the game being niche, and the community being small, it doesn't have that effect a lot, outside of perhaps some specific communities

threads.formatted_text:
--- Thread (13 messages, 2021-05-14) ---
[2021-05-14 15:07] Deleted User (Deleted User): I'm still a noob but does anybody have a theory, why does the bot sometimes leave valuable units to be breached in mid-game?
[2021-05-14 15:07] awaclus: because it's an idiot
[2021-05-14 15:09] awaclus: buying the forcefield there as the bot would have been very trivially better than not buying it
[2021-05-14 15:09] awaclus: and probably buying more prompt defense to save the boreholes would have been good too
[2021-05-14 15:09] awaclus: instead of buying a third tesla
[2021-05-14 15:09] siepu: That's interesting in my games bot does absolutely everething to make sure patrolers don't die
[2021-05-14 15:10] awaclus: oh wait, you had the pixies too
[2021-05-14 15:10] awaclus: so a single forcefield wouldn't have been enough
[2021-05-14 15:10] awaclus: well, regardless, it's clear that saving a centurion is way better than buying a third tesla
[2021-05-14 15:12] awaclus: sometimes the bot AI crashes and is replaced by the bot that literally just does random things, it's possible that happened here
[2021-05-14 15:13] awaclus: but on the other hand, sometimes Master Bot is perfectly capable of being an idiot even when it's working properly
[2021-05-14 15:14] siepu: True but again usually the bad things are not what happened here
[2021-05-14 15:15] siepu: So it might have crashed

[2021-09-23 01:24] iminabearsuit: You can create a match vs any computer, and resign immediately. And then on the game over screen you can click the Analyze button, which can work as a Hot Seat mode.
[2021-09-23 01:24] iminabearsuit: Alternatively the Analyze Blueprint Set on any replay
[2021-09-23 02:55] doofmaster: Very helpful and :Prompt: response, thank you!

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: You can create a match vs any computer, and resign immediately. And then on the game over screen you can click the Analyze button, which can work as a Hot Seat mode.

messages.author: iminabearsuit
messages.content: Alternatively the Analyze Blueprint Set on any replay

messages.author: doofmaster
messages.content: Very helpful and :Prompt: response, thank you!

[2023-03-12 14:55] sano712 (sano): I had that issue with the emoji window until recently. I'm assume you're using a mac
[2023-03-12 14:56] sharpobject: hi Steel, D is bound to Drone
[2023-03-12 14:56] sano712 (sano): Go to system settings, app shortcuts, and then create a shortcut for the emoji window and bind it so something you'll never use ingame, such as cmd+ctrl+E
[2023-03-12 14:56] sano712 (sano): If you Google there's an explanation on how to do this, I'm on my phone RN so can't do it
[2023-03-12 14:57] sharpobject: Thanks. I really need to fix the dictation though. The emoji picker is fine
[2023-03-12 14:57] sharpobject: my shortcut in settings for dictation is shift+cmd+ctrl+option+I. but in Prismata it's D for whatever reason.
[2023-03-12 15:00] sharpobject: I don't have "app shortcuts" in system settings on Mac OS 13.2.1
[2023-03-12 15:04] sharpobject: ok, sano was correct, E also gives an emoji picker if I hit E during a game
[2023-03-12 15:14] sano712 (sano): I imagine the same solution should work?
